summary: MIN_ENTRY_CONFIDENCE=0.60/0.65 gate exists in code and is enforced at runtime, BUT was NOT enforced at time of trades 223-224. Root cause: code/config version mismatch between deployments on 2026-04-10.

check_entry_gates_usage.evidence: bot_core.py:632 calls check_entry_gates() for model_bridge entries. If gate fails, logs 'BRIDGE GATE REJECT [check_entry_gates]' and blocks entry.

min_entry_confidence_loaded.evidence: core/risk.py:41 loads from env with default 0.60. .env:47 overrides to 0.65. Current deployed config: MIN_ENTRY_CONFIDENCE=0.65

trade_223_mlb_ari_phi_20260410.status: BEFORE 18:29:31 restart — NO check_entry_gates rejection logged, trade OPENED successfully
trade_223_mlb_ari_phi_20260410.after_restart_verdict: WOULD BE REJECTED: logs at 18:33:15 show 'confidence_too_low:0.36:0.600'

trade_224_mlb_laa_cin_20260410.status: BEFORE 18:29:31 restart — NO check_entry_gates rejection logged, trade OPENED successfully
trade_224_mlb_laa_cin_20260410.after_restart_verdict: WOULD BE REJECTED: logs at 18:33:15 show 'confidence_too_low:0.3382:0.600'

trades_223_224_before_after.summary: Both trades violated 0.60/0.65 floor but were allowed to open. Same markets rejected with gate message 1+ hour later.

17:28:59_restart.diagnostic: min_conf=0.25 (this is model_bridge gate, not entry gate)

17:44:07.logs: BRIDGE GATE PASS (from model_bridge), then BRIDGE OPEN trade=223. NO 'BRIDGE GATE REJECT [check_entry_gates]' message.

17:47:37.logs: BRIDGE GATE PASS (from model_bridge), then BRIDGE OPEN trade=224. NO 'BRIDGE GATE REJECT [check_entry_gates]' message.

18:29:31_restart.change: check_entry_gates enforcement appears to have been enabled or fixed

18:33:15.event: Same market slugs (ari-phi, laa-cin) receive new intents from model_bridge
18:33:15.logs: BRIDGE GATE PASS with new confidence values, then IMMEDIATELY BRIDGE GATE REJECT [check_entry_gates] with 'confidence_too_low:X:0.600'

root_cause_analysis.hypothesis: Code/config version mismatch. The deployment running from 17:28:59 did not have check_entry_gates MIN_ENTRY_CONFIDENCE validation, OR it was disabled.
evidence: check_entry_gates() exists in current code and includes MIN_ENTRY_CONFIDENCE check at lines 165-168
However, trades 223-224 opening without rejection indicates this check was not active before 18:29:31
After 18:29:31 restart, identical market slugs with similar confidence values ARE rejected with the gate message
No code deployment logs found, but timing strongly suggests 17:28-18:29 window saw config or code change
root_cause_analysis.security_gap_identified: model_bridge has MIN_CONFIDENCE=0.25 hardcoded. If check_entry_gates is not called or not loaded, trades can slip through with 0.25 < confidence < 0.60 range.

explicit_yes_no_answers.is_real_bug: YES — the gate exists and works, but was NOT enforced at the time of trades 223-224. This is a runtime enforcement failure due to version mismatch or conditional disablement.

next_steps: Verify what code was actually running at 17:28:59 vs 18:29:31 on 2026-04-10. Check deployment logs, git tags, or runtime config snapshots.
Confirm whether check_entry_gates MIN_ENTRY_CONFIDENCE logic was recently added or modified (commit dates, PR history).
Establish deployment versioning/git tag enforcement so all runtime instances have matching code/config.
Add startup diagnostic logging for MIN_ENTRY_CONFIDENCE and check_entry_gates presence/status to catch future version mismatches.
Review if hardcoded model_bridge MIN_CONFIDENCE=0.25 should be harmonized with MIN_ENTRY_CONFIDENCE or if dual-gate is intentional.
